metadata.title: Detect and Name Dangerous Middle Tasks Before Delegating

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: The five-capacity audit for spotting dangerous-middle tasks is exactly the discipline I skip. I want to run it on three tasks from my own project before delegating. Walk me through: (1) scoring a SQL query task for IJ around index-hint interpretation; (2) scoring a graph-traversal task for TO to enforce a runtime limit; (3) confirming a docstring-generation task really is near-zero across PA, PF, TO, IJ, and EI so I can delegate it freely as the contrast case.

props.command: The five-capacity audit for spotting dangerous-middle tasks is exactly the discipline I skip. I want to run it on three tasks from my own project before delegating. Walk me through: (1) scoring a SQL query task for IJ around index-hint interpretation; (2) scoring a graph-traversal task for TO to enforce a runtime limit; (3) confirming a docstring-generation task really is near-zero across PA, PF, TO, IJ, and EI so I can delegate it freely as the contrast case.
props.segment: Detect and Name Dangerous Middle Tasks

beats.narration_text: The task looks like pattern work — write a sort, build a query, pick an algorithm — but each one hides a supervisory requirement Claude cannot supply.

The dangerous middle is not a list of bug categories. It is the region where surface-pattern fluency overrides the need for judgment. Naming which capacity is required before delegating is the countermove.
We analyze three tasks for dangerous-middle risk using the five supervisory capacity framework.

props.command:
claude "Analyze for dangerous-middle risk using five capacities:
  1. GPA sort with tie-breaking
  2. SQL query with index hint
  3. Graph algorithm with runtime limit
For each: which supervisory capacity is REQUIRED?
Why is it hidden in pattern-looking work?"

beats.narration_text: GPA sort: PA required — tie-case probe is a PA move. PF required — alphabetical by last_name is a formulation gap. SQL query: IJ required — index hint interpretation. Graph algorithm: TO required — runtime limit enforcement.

props.code:
# Dangerous Middle Audit

## Task 1: GPA sort with tie-breaking
PF REQUIRED: 'alphabetical by last_name' = formulation gap
  (unstated: ascending or descending?)
PA REQUIRED: tie-case probe is a PA move
  (no tie case = bug invisible at generation time)
Danger: HIGH — two hidden capacities

## Task 2: SQL query with index hint
IJ REQUIRED: query plan interpretation
  (hint may be correct syntax but wrong for this schema)
Danger: MEDIUM — one hidden capacity

## Task 3: Graph algorithm with runtime limit
TO REQUIRED: runtime limit enforcement
  (Claude cannot enforce a wall-clock constraint)
Danger: MEDIUM — one hidden capacity

beats.narration_text: Three tasks, three danger profiles. GPA sort: PA and PF required — two capacities hidden in pattern work. SQL query: IJ required for query plan interpretation. Graph algorithm: TO required to enforce the runtime constraint. None of these appear in the task description.
We run the same analysis on generate a docstring for this function — verify it scores near zero as the contrast case.

props.command:
claude "Analyze for dangerous-middle risk:
  Task: generate a docstring for this function
Which supervisory capacities are required?
Is this pure pattern work or does it hide supervision?"

beats.narration_text: Docstring generation: PF not required — no formulation gap. PA minimal — output is self-evident. TO not required. IJ not required. EI not required. Near-zero dangerous-middle score. This is pure pattern work — delegate freely.
The dangerous middle audit takes two minutes before delegation and prevents the category of failure where Claude's fluent output masks the judgment it could not supply.
Next: measure the fluency versus correctness gap on a known-buggy output.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Detect and Name Dangerous Middle Tasks Before Delegating] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
